name: Geoffrey Hinton
title: VP & Engineering Fellow at Google, Professor Emeritus at University of Toronto
about: Renowned computer scientist and cognitive psychologist, widely recognized as the 'Godfather of Deep Learning' and 'Godfather of AI'. Pioneered backpropagation and convolutional neural networks. Co-recipient of the 2018 Turing Award for conceptual and engineering breakthroughs that have made deep neural networks a critical component of computing. Led groundbreaking research in artificial neural networks, machine learning, and AI safety.

projects.name: AlexNet
projects.description: Revolutionary convolutional neural network that won the ImageNet competition in 2012, sparking the deep learning revolution.

projects.name: Backpropagation Algorithm
projects.description: Fundamental algorithm for training neural networks, enabling the modern deep learning era.

projects.name: Boltzmann Machines
projects.description: Energy-based models for learning probability distributions, foundation for many modern generative models.

projects.name: Capsule Networks
projects.description: Alternative to convolutional networks that preserve spatial relationships between features.

projects.name: Transformer Architecture
projects.description: Attention-based neural network architecture that revolutionized NLP and became foundation for GPT models.

projects.name: Deep Belief Networks
projects.description: Hierarchical generative models composed of restricted Boltzmann machines, enabling efficient learning.

projects.name: Neural Machine Translation
projects.description: End-to-end neural network approach to machine translation, surpassing traditional statistical methods.

projects.name: AI Safety Research
projects.description: Research on ensuring AI systems remain aligned with human values and beneficial to humanity.

availability: Available for academic collaborations and AI policy discussions
